[.]NETアセンブリをPythonから操作するライブラリ「pythonnet」とMCPサーバーを組み合わせ、[.]NETマルウェア解析の反復作業をAIアシスタントに肩代わりさせた事例。APT28がC2フレームワーク「Covenant」の拡張機能で、クラウドストレージKoofr／Filen経由のC2通信を実装した検体が確認され、その解析を題材に、[.]NET中間言語のオペコード列から暗号化文字列の復号ルーチンを特定し、鍵抽出とldstrオペランド書き換えまでPython側で自動化した上で、AIアシスタントと連携して解析を進めるアプローチが紹介されています。  同アプローチはMCPサーバーとして実装され、解析ツール群をAI側から自律的に呼び出せる構成でオープンソース提供（GitHub）されています。  【要点の整理】  ・出典：Sekoia TDRチームが2026年4月16日付で公開した技術ブログ。APT28運用のCovenant検体を題材に、[.]NET解析の自動化手法とMCPサーバー構成を記載。  ・検体の概要：APT28（ロシアGRU第85特別任務センター帰属と評価）が運用するCovenant検体。C2Bridge拡張点を用いてクラウドストレージKoofr／Filenへのファイル送受信だけでC2通信を完結させる独自実装との指摘。プライマリアクセス喪失時の再感染用としてC++製フォールバックBeardShell、キーロギングと情報窃取に特化したSlimAgentへの言及あり。  ・従来解析の摩擦点：暗号化文字列の個別復号や関数名の逐次リネームが反復的で、[.]NETデコンパイラdnSpyもコード書き換えには向かないため解析を遅らせていたとの説明。  ・自動化の中核：pythonnetで[.]NETアセンブリを操作し、ILオペコード列の署名パターンで復号関数を特定。ldsfld命令から鍵名、静的コンストラクタ（[.]cctor）で鍵値を取得し、ldstrオペランドを復号値に置換、復号関数呼び出しをnop化するパッチまで自動化する構成。  ・公開ツール「RePythonNET-MCP」：セッション管理・メタデータ抽出・ナビゲーション・解析・IL検査・デコンパイル・パッチの7カテゴリ計38ツールがGitHubで公開。「pythonnetで該当ファイルを解析。推測はせず、C2が見つかったら送って」の3文プロンプトでAI側が検体ロード〜C2特定を自律実行する事例をスクリーンショットで提示。  同チームはAI連携解析を、万能策ではなく反復作業の一部を肩代わりする実用的な手段と位置付け。[.]NETリバースエンジニアリングのエコシステムは依然として薄いとの観察のもと、解析者の反復作業の一部を軽減するOSSが共有された形。  詳細はリンク先の記事を参照。 blog.sekoia.io/apt28-to-repy…

⚠️ Apple kassiert 30 Prozent von jeder App die im Store verkauft wird. Für diese 30 Prozent bekommst du angeblich Sicherheit. Ein Review-Team das prüft. Eine Marke die dafür steht, dass nicht jeder Betrüger seine Malware in dein iPhone kippen kann.  Das ist die Geschichte die Apple verkauft.  Die Realität ist @glove der sein komplettes Rentenvermögen an eine Fake-Ledger-App verloren hat. Die App lag im offiziellen Apple App Store. Sie hat den Review-Prozess bestanden. Sie hat Downloads gesammelt. Sie hat Seed-Phrases abgegriffen.  Und sie war nicht die erste.  2023 war es eine Fake-Trezor-App. Ein User hat 1,5 Millionen Dollar in #Bitcoin verloren. Apple hat die App entfernt - nach dem Schaden. Der Review-Prozess hat sie durchgewunken. Keine Entschädigung. Kein öffentliches Eingeständnis.  2024 wieder Fake-Wallets. 2025 wieder. Jedes Mal das gleiche Muster. Jemand verliert alles, die Krypto-Twitter-Community schreit, Apple nimmt die App nach 48 Stunden runter, und das nächste Team schiebt die gleiche Malware unter neuem Namen rein.  Das Review-Team das für deine 30 Prozent bezahlt wird findet Emoji-Verletzungen in Sekunden. Aber eine Wallet-App die Seeds sammelt rutscht durch.  Warum?  Weil Apple keine Haftung trägt. Die AGB sagen ausdrücklich: nicht unser Problem. Du stimmst zu wenn du das Gerät einschaltest. @glove hat zugestimmt als er 16 Jahre alt war.  Jetzt die unangenehme Seite. Self-Custody heißt: du bist die Bank. Kein Support, kein Callcenter, kein Rollback wenn du falsch klickst. Ledger verkauft dir Sicherheit durch Hardware, aber die App die du für die Ersteinrichtung nutzt ist Einfallstor.  Zwei Welten die dich beide ruinieren können. Die Bank die dein Geld einfriert weil sie kann. Der App Store der eine Fake-Wallet durchwinkt weil er schläft.  Was machst du?

Chrome 제로데이 CVE-2026-5281 — WebGPU Dawn use-after-free, 실제 공격 진행 중  Google이 Chrome 보안 업데이트를 긴급 배포했습니다. 이번에 패치된 CVE-2026-5281은 WebGPU의 핵심 구현체인 Dawn에서 발견된 use-after-free 취약점으로, 이미 실제 공격에 활용되고 있습니다.  --  취약점 개요  • CVE ID: CVE-2026-5281 • 영향 컴포넌트: Dawn (Chrome의 WebGPU 표준 구현체) • 취약점 유형: Use-After-Free (UAF) • 심각도: High • 패치 버전: Chrome 146.0.7680.177/178 (Windows/macOS), 146.0.7680.177 (Linux) • 상태: 실제 공격에 활용 중 (in the wild)  --  Dawn과 WebGPU란?  Dawn은 Google이 개발한 오픈소스 크로스플랫폼 WebGPU 구현체입니다. WebGPU는 웹 브라우저에서 GPU에 직접 접근할 수 있게 해주는 차세대 그래픽 API로, WebGL의 후속입니다.  문제는 이 API가 GPU 메모리에 저수준으로 접근한다는 점입니다. 여기서 메모리 안전 버그가 발생하면 공격 표면이 매우 넓어집니다.  --  공격 시나리오  use-after-free는 이미 해제된 메모리 영역을 다시 참조하는 버그입니다. 공격자는 이를 악용해:  • 조작된 HTML 페이지를 제작 • 피해자가 해당 페이지를 방문하면 렌더러 프로세스 침투 • 렌더러 프로세스에서 임의 코드 실행 • 샌드박스 탈출 시 시스템 전체 장악 가능  유포 경로는 다양합니다: • 피싱 메일에 포함된 링크 • 악성 광고 (malvertising) • 정상 사이트의 리다이렉트 체인 악용 • 워터링 홀 공격 (특정 타겟이 방문하는 사이트 변조)  --  2026년 Chrome 제로데이 타임라인  올해만 벌써 네 번째 Chrome 제로데이입니다:  • 2월: CVE-2026-2441 — CSS 컴포넌트 use-after-free • 3월: CVE-2026-3909 — 고위험 취약점 • 3월: CVE-2026-3910 — 고위험 취약점 • 4월: CVE-2026-5281 — WebGPU Dawn use-after-free ← 지금 이것  4개월간 4건. 공격자들이 브라우저를 핵심 공격 벡터로 집중 타겟팅하고 있다는 명확한 신호입니다.  --  CISA 긴급 대응  미국 CISA(사이버보안 및 인프라 보안국)는 CVE-2026-5281을 KEV(Known Exploited Vulnerabilities) 카탈로그에 즉시 등재했습니다.  • 연방기관 패치 마감: 2026년 4월 15일 • 민간 기업 및 개인에게도 즉각 업데이트 강력 권고 • KEV 등재 = "이 취약점은 이론이 아니라 실전"이라는 의미  --  지금 당장 해야 할 것  1. Chrome 업데이트: 설정 → Chrome 정보 → 146.0.7680.178 확인 2. Chromium 브라우저 전체 업데이트: Edge, Brave, Opera, Vivaldi 3. 임시 완화: chrome://flags에서 WebGPU 비활성화 4. 조직 내 브라우저 버전 일괄 점검 5. 의심스러운 링크 클릭 주의 — 특히 GPU 관련 데모/테스트 페이지  --  보안은 패치 속도가 전부입니다. 제로데이는 발견 시점이 아니라 패치 적용 시점까지가 위험 구간입니다. 지금 브라우저를 업데이트하고, 주변에도 알려주세요.

중국 칩 수출 — 인터뷰의 가장 뜨거운 논쟁  인터뷰어 입장 (수출 제한 지지): - Anthropic의 Mythos 모델: 모든 주요 OS·브라우저에서 수천 개 고위험 취약점 발견 - OpenBSD (27년간 제로데이 없던 OS)에서도 취약점 발견 - 더 많은 컴퓨트 = 더 강한 공격 모델 훈련·대규모 배포 가능 - 미국이 먼저 도달해서 방어할 시간을 버는 게 중요  젠슨의 반론 — 다섯 가지 포인트:  1) "이미 충분한 컴퓨트가 있다" - 중국은 세계 2위 컴퓨팅 시장, 주류 칩 60% 이상 제조 - 세계 AI 연구자의 50%가 중국인 - 에너지가 풍부 → 7nm 칩을 10배 더 투입하면 됨 - 화웨이 역대 최대 매출 기록 — "수백만 개 출하, Anthropic보다 훨씬 많다" - "고스트 데이터센터가 있다. 전력 공급 완비된 채 비어있는 곳이"  2) "7nm에 갇혀있다는 전제가 틀렸다" - Hopper → Blackwell이 50배인데, 트랜지스터 자체는 75% 향상뿐 - "무어의 법칙은 죽었다. 아키텍처, 컴퓨터 과학, 네트워킹이 진짜 레버" - AI 발전의 대부분은 하드웨어가 아니라 알고리즘 혁신에서 왔다 - DeepSeek은 "사소한 진전이 아니다"  3) "미국 칩 산업이 세계 2위 시장을 양보하는 건 국가 안보에 해롭다" - 중국은 전 세계 기술 산업의 40% - "이 시장을 내주면 자체 생태계를 키우고, 미래 AI 모델이 미국 기술 스택이 아닌 다른 스택에 최적화" - 통신 산업의 교훈: 정책으로 시장을 내줬더니 자국 통신조차 통제 불능  4) "중국 오픈소스 생태계를 질식시키면 안 된다" - 세계 최대 오픈소스 기여국, 세계 최대 오픈 모델 기여국이 중국 - 현재 미국 기술 스택 위에서 개발 중 → 이 관계를 끊으면 두 개의 분리된 생태계 탄생 - "폐쇄 생태계는 미국 스택, 오픈소스 생태계는 중국 스택 — 이건 끔찍한 결과"  5) "대화가 필요하다" - 미중 AI 연구자 간 대화가 완전히 단절된 상태가 가장 위험 - "적이 아닌 경쟁자. 승리하되, 대화는 해야 한다"  젠슨의 핵심 메시지: "AI는 5층 케이크다. 에너지, 칩, 컴퓨팅 스택, 모델, 애플리케이션. 미국은 5개 층 모두에서 이겨야 한다. 한 층(모델)만 보면서 다른 층(칩 산업)을 희생시키는 건 패배주의적 사고다."

예상.  초고압 케이블 LS에코에너지: KEMA 230kV 인증 획득, 북미 초고압 케이블 시장 2027년 79억 달러 규모.  나프타 대란 국일제지(+18.99%), 무림페이퍼(+21.04%). 한화토탈 원료 고갈로 공급 중단 선언, 석화 원료 부족 → 종이 패키징 대체 수요.  방산 비츠로셀: 미 국방부 특수 배터리 공급 논의. 코스닥150 편입 기대감.  실적 모멘텀 코나아이: 1Q 영업이익 246억(+94% YoY). 국내·수출 동반 성장.  광통신 빛과전자, 광전자, 코위버, 우리넷, 대한광통신. 라이트카운팅 2026년 이더넷 광 트랜시버+CPO 시장 260억 달러 전망(+60%). 엔비디아 젠슨 황 "향후 5년 내 데이터센터 내 모든 고대역폭 연결 광 전환."   단, 빛과전자·대한광통신은 이미 투자경고/위험 구간.  중국산 셀룰러 모듈 규제 스마트홈 관련주: 현대에이치티, 삼진, 머큐리, 폴라리스오피스, 아남전자, 엠젠솔루션, 에브리봇, 솔루엠.  테마 2연타 상한가 종목들의 과열 구간 진입 확인 보안·양자 1차 밴드는 이미 거래일 2일차 진입했고, 케이씨에스·SGA솔루션즈·드림시큐리티·파인텍·라온시큐어가 나란히 투자경고 예고에 걸림. 테마의 지속성은 유효하지만 개별 종목 단기 과열은 별개 이슈. 차트로 검증하고 들어가야 함.  블랙록이 신흥국 비중확대 근거로 한국을 꼽은 건 큰 그림에서 중요한 시그널. 외국인 수급이 3일 연속 순매수 지속 중이고 이번 주 코스피 3% 이상 상승분의 상당 부분이 외국인·기관 매수에서 나옴. 반도체 중심 이익 추정치 상향으로 12개월 선행 PER 7.4배로 여전히 낮음.  이란 전쟁 종전 협상은 21일이 분수령임. 합의든 연장이든 결과에 따라 유가·건설·재건주 방향성 결정됨. 미리 한쪽 포지션으로 다 몰지 말고 양방향 시나리오 준비하는 게 안전함.  오픈AI-삼성 HBM4 비공개 회동은 '메모리 주도권 재편' 신호임. 기존 엔비디아 중심 구도에서 AI 하이퍼스케일러가 직접 메모리 공급망 확보하는 흐름. 삼성 소부장 밸류체인이 당분간 더 탄탄해질 근거.  SDT 관련주(무림P&P, 시공테크, 나우IB, 파인텍)는 연내 IPO가 변수. 상장 전 지분가치 재평가는 유효하지만, 상장 후 시세 반영 차익실현 구간 주의. 엑스포·간담회·실적 발표 등 일정에 맞춰 변동성 커짐.  4.21 삼천당제약 애널리스트 간담회(경구용 인슐린 PK 데이터), 4.22 AACR 2026 발표 지속, 4.23 SK하이닉스 1분기 실적 발표(영업이익 40조 전망), 4.27 스테이블코인법 상정 예정. 다음 주 이벤트 드리븐 구간임. 미리 체크하고 들어갈지 말지 결정하기.  투자경고·위험 종목군(빛과전자, 한국첨단소재, 광전자, 이노인스트루먼트, 우리로, 기가레인)은 이미 한 번 털린 자리. 반등 노리고 들어가면 더 깊은 구간 만날 수 있음.

What O’Connor is doing is the Gish Gallop. Fire seven claims in 90 seconds, knowing each one takes five minutes to unpack. That’s not an argument, that’s a rhetorical DDOS. Audit them one at a time and they fall: •Simon of Cyrene: no gospel claims Jesus carried it the whole way. Sequence, not contradiction. •Two thieves: Luke records a shift mid-crucifixion. Matthew/Mark give the opening scene. •Temple curtain: Luke 23:44-46 places the tear before the death. Same passage. •Last words: multiple statements from the cross is standard reading, not competing “finals.” The textually serious issues (day of death, hour of crucifixion) have real scholarly answers in calendar reckoning and Roman vs Jewish time. O’Connor knows this. He’s not doing exegesis, he’s doing theater.

It is often interesting to observe ongoing discussions about the limited appreciation of Digital Forensics and Incident Response (DFIR) within the legal profession, particularly in parts of Africa. This challenge is not confined to one country; it is equally evident in Ghana and across the region. In my experience, many legal practitioners possess only a foundational understanding: if any of cybersecurity and digital forensics. Yet, as technology continues to shape modern investigations and litigation, the relevance of digital evidence cannot be overstated.  There is a growing need for lawyers to expand their expertise beyond traditional forms of evidence and develop competence in digital forensics. Doing so will not only strengthen legal practice but also position practitioners to effectively navigate the evolving landscape of cyber-related cases.  The legal profession must adapt. Embracing digital forensics is no longer optional it is essential for staying relevant in today’s digital age.

YARA remains the gold standard for malware pattern matching across the DFIR community.  At its core, YARA is a rule-based identification engine that lets you describe malware families, threat actor tooling, or suspicious artifacts using strings, hex patterns, regex, and logical conditions. Whether you're hunting through disk images, memory dumps, or live filesystems, YARA rules give you precise, repeatable detection logic that scales from single-sample analysis to enterprise-wide threat hunting.  Key capabilities: • Write custom rules using ASCII/wide strings, hex byte sequences, regex patterns, and boolean logic • Integrate seamlessly with VirusTotal, Velociraptor, KAPE, ClamAV, and virtually every major security platform • Use yarGen to auto-generate rules from malware samples via intelligent string extraction and frequency analysis  Real-world scenario: During a ransomware investigation, you extract unique mutex names and API call sequences from the binary. Write a YARA rule targeting those artifacts, deploy it via Velociraptor across 5,000 endpoints, and identify three additional compromised hosts within minutes—hosts that traditional AV missed.  YARA is maintained by VirusTotal and has become the lingua franca of malware research. If you're doing detection engineering or malware analysis without YARA in your toolkit, you're working with one hand tied behind your back.  #DFIRTools #DigitalForensics

🔐 Velociraptor en DFIR & CSIRT: visibilidad, velocidad y control absoluto 🔐  En organizaciones con cientos o miles de endpoints, la respuesta a incidentes y el threat hunting eficaz requieren plataformas centralizadas, precisas y con capacidad de acción remota. Velociraptor es una plataforma open-source para recolección y análisis forense a escala. Su lenguaje de consultas (VQL) y arquitectura cliente-servidor permiten a equipos DFIR/CSIRT investigar, contener y erradicar amenazas con rapidez y trazabilidad.   1. Beneficios:  • Investigación y contención más rápidas (reducción de MTTD/MTTR). • Evidencia estandarizada y auditable para escalado y acciones legales. • Reutilización de hunting mediante consultas VQL y artefactos versionados. • Coordinación eficiente entre equipos DFIR / CSIRT y orquestación centralizada.  2 .Buenas prácticas y recomendaciones  • Definir artefactos base y plantillas VQL por TTP (MITRE ATT&CK). • Integrar con SIEM / SOAR para ingestión de alertas y ejecución automatizada. • Establecer control de acceso, retención, cifrado y custodia de evidencia. • Monitorear carga en endpoints/red y ajustar periodicidad/tamaño de colecciones. • Probar en staging y mantener playbooks versionados.  3. Qué hice en laboratorio  • Despliegue de servidor y cliente con configuración dedicada. • Validación de conectividad y telemetría desde el dashboard. • Hunt con "Generic.System.Pstree" para mapear cadenas de procesos. • Ejecución remota de PowerShell ("netstat -anob") para correlacionar puertos ↔ procesos en vivo. • Revisión de resultados y preparación de un Process Hunt reutilizable.  4. Resultados   •  Menor tiempo de investigación y contención. •  Evidencia consistente para auditoría y legal hold. •  Mejora continua del hunting mediante librería VQL reutilizable. ✅  - Articulo completo en el primer comentario  #DFIR #DigitalForensics #CyberSecurity #IncidentResponse #WindowsForensics #ThreatHunting #ThreatIntelligence #BlueTeam #SOC #SOCAnalyst #ThreatDetection #CyberDefense #SecurityOperations #ForensicAnalysis #DFIRCommunity #Infosec #CyberThreats #LogAnalysis #ThreatInvestigation #SecurityMonitoring #MalwareAnalysis #LivingOffTheLand #TeamViewer #WindowsSecurity #ThreatAnalysis #CyberIncident

We keep talking about detection, response, and threat intelligence — as if the problem is speed. Detect faster. Respond faster. Patch faster.  But speed does not fix exposure. Because exposure is not a timing issue. It is a design issue. If a system allows execution across trust boundaries, if privilege is not structurally constrained, if identity is not consistently enforced —  then no amount of detection will change the outcome.  It will only change how quickly we realize the system is already compromised. And that is not security. That is delayed awareness.  Real security begins when the system itself removes the possibility of certain actions. Not detects them. Not reacts to them. Removes them. That is the difference between defense and architecture.  #Cybersecurity #Infrastructure #SecurityArchitecture

EPA, FBI, CISA, NSA Issue Joint Cybersecurity Advisory to Water System Regarding Iranian-Affiliated Cyber Attacks  Advisory contains information to promote resilience, ensure safety of Americans drinking water  U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and National Security Agency (NSA) issued a joint advisory warning to U.S. organizations, including those in the water sector, for an urgent and ongoing Iranian-affiliated cybersecurity threat. U.S. organizations are experiencing exploitation and, in some cases, disruption of commonly used operational technology at drinking water and wastewater systems that are diligently working to ensure that Americans can rely on clean and safe water.  “Cybersecurity threats are a serious concern for our nation’s water infrastructure, including the communities, businesses, hospitals, and other critical infrastructure sectors that rely on these critical lifeline services,” said EPA Assistant Administrator for Water Jess Kramer. “Water systems are encouraged to stay informed and work to adopt cybersecurity best practices.”  “Cyberattacks on drinking water and wastewater systems directly threaten public health and community resilience. A single breach can disrupt treatment or introduce contaminants, damage equipment, and erode public trust, said EPA Assistant Administrator for Enforcement and Compliance Assurance Jeffrey A. Hall. “EPA enforcement safeguards our nation’s critical infrastructure, including water systems, by ensuring compliance with applicable safety, maintenance, resilience, and security requirements and by rapidly correcting vulnerabilities. We also work with our law enforcement partners to disrupt attacks and to hold those responsible accountable. Our national security depends on water systems not only taking security seriously but also immediately reporting any incidents and working with our investigators to address them while protecting the public.”  The joint cybersecurity advisory includes information to help water systems identify specific vulnerabilities that are being exploited and take concrete steps to strengthen cyber resilience. It also underscores that the water sector remains an attractive target and continues to face threats from groups seeking to disrupt U.S. critical infrastructure. As a result of the recent cybersecurity exploitation, organizations from multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors have reported disruptions including configuration wiping, software-based mechanical sensor tampering, and disruption of human machine interfaces (HMIs). This activity has resulted in operational disruption and financial loss.  epa.gov/newsreleases/epa-fbi…  Iranian-Affiliated Cyber Actors Exploit Programmable Logic Controllers Across US Critical Infrastructure  Iran-affiliated advanced persistent threat (APT) actors are conducting exploitation activity targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices, including programmable logic controllers (PLCs) manufactured by Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley. This activity has led to PLC disruptions across several U.S. critical infrastructure sectors through malicious interactions with the project file and manipulation of data on human machine interface (HMI) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) displays, resulting in operational disruption and financial loss.  U.S. organizations should urgently review the tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) and indicators of compromise (IOCs) in this advisory for indications of current or historical activity on their networks, and apply the recommendations listed in the Mitigations section of this advisory to reduce the risk of compromise.  cisa.gov/news-events/cyberse…  Iranian Cyber Actors May Target Vulnerable U.S. Networks and Entities of Interest  The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the Department of Defense Cyber Crime Center (DC3), and the National Security Agency (NSA) (hereafter referred to as the authoring agencies) strongly urge organizations to remain vigilant for potential targeted cyber activity against U.S. critical infrastructure and other U.S. entities by Iranian-affiliated cyber actors.  fbi.gov/file-repository/cybe…  US warns of Iran-affiliated cyber-attacks on critical infrastructure across country  Security agencies say municipalities should watch out for unusual activity, especially in water and energy sectors  Top government security agencies issued a warning of Iran-affiliated cyber-attacks on critical infrastructure across the US on Tuesday. In a joint statement, the agencies said municipalities should be on the lookout for unusual activity, especially around water and energy facilities.  “Cyberattacks on drinking water and wastewater systems directly threaten public health and community resilience,” Jeffrey Hall, an assistant administrator for enforcement and compliance assurance for the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), said in a statement. “A single breach can disrupt treatment or introduce contaminants, damage equipment, and erode public trust.”  The notice did not detail whether specific facilities had been targeted or any damage had been sustained. The agencies that issued the joint advisory include the EPA, the FBI, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, the National Security Agency, the Department of Energy and the US Cyber Command.  The warning came as Donald Trump has stepped up violent rhetoric against Iran, posting on social media in the early morning hours on Tuesday that “a whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again” if the Middle Eastern country didn’t give into his demands.  Shortly before Trump’s Tuesday evening deadline, the US and Iran agreed to a provisional ceasefire, with Tehran agreeing to reopen the strait of Hormuz if the US suspended strikes.  Iran has been accused for years of carrying out cyber-attacks on various countries, including a massive power outage in Turkey in 2015 and several possible breaches of Israeli government websites in 2022. The US alleged an Iran-affiliated group known as “CyberAv3ngers” carried out a campaign against it in 2023 that compromised at least 75 devices in multiple infrastructure sectors.  Iran has also accused the US and Israel of carrying out several cyber-attacks against it, including on its nuclear centrifuges and its weapons systems.  Tuesday’s advisory alleged that the hackers are backed by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. It said the attacks were focusing on a widely used device called a “programmable logic controller” that’s specifically made by the company Rockwell Automation. Siemens, which also makes these devices, was not named.  The government agencies urged any municipalities using these devices to make sure they are not connected to the internet.  theguardian.com/world/2026/a…

sample_tweets.text: 🇪🇺🤦 EUROPA, WAS MACHT DU DA WIEDER?  Die neue Altersverifikations-App der EU. Frisch ausgerollt. Am selben Tag schon zerlegt.  Das Verfahren ist lächerlich: PIN löschen. Neue PIN setzen. Zugriff auf die bestehenden Anmeldedaten bleibt erhalten. Andere "Schutzmaßnahmen" sind editierbare Einstellungen. Kein Werkzeug nötig. Kein Exploit. Die App zerlegt sich praktisch selbst.  Und das Ding wurde mit Millionen Steuergeld gebaut.  @AdGuard hat Wochen vorher gewarnt: "Diese Gesetze sind in Wahrheit ein Geschenk an Betrüger und ein Albtraum für deine Privatsphäre." 400 Cybersecurity-Experten haben einen offenen Brief unterzeichnet. Man hat sie ignoriert und trotzdem durchgedrückt.   @TFTC21 hat die Mechanik offengelegt: 26,3 Mio. USD Meta-Lobbying in den USA. Die Gesetze regulieren nicht Social Media. Sie regulieren dein Betriebssystem. 2 Mrd. USD in Grants und PACs nachverfolgt. Der Sponsor des Louisiana-Gesetzes hat bestätigt, dass der Meta-Lobbyist den Text geschrieben hat. In Brüssel läuft dasselbe Drehbuch mit weniger Transparenz.  @BLkhoih96132 zum UK-Vorbild: "Der Online Safety Act ist ein totales Versagen." Kleine Seiten schließen. Das Netz wird age-gated. Die Ziele werden nicht erreicht. Die EU schaut auf das Desaster und sagt: gute Idee, bauen wir nach.  @EFF direkt: "Was für ein schräger Unsinn läuft da bei Firmen, die dein Alter schätzen oder deinen Ausweis für Altersverifikation verlangen." @Skarka47 knapper: "EU-Theater ist cool, bis dir einfällt, dass sie Altersverifikations-Gesetze am Start hat."  Jetzt die Frage, die in Brüssel keiner beantworten will: Wie viele Millionen hat dieses Ding gekostet? Ausschreibungen, Consultants, Pilotphasen, Evaluierungsgremien, Folgestudien, Sitzungsgelder. Am Ende eine PIN, die jedes Kind in drei Klicks umgeht.  Deine ID. Dein Gesicht. Deine biometrischen Daten. In einer zentralen Datenbank, "geschützt" durch eine PIN, die du selbst überschreiben kannst. Studien sagen: Minderjährige werden durch solche Systeme 35 bis 51 Mal anfälliger für Identitätsdiebstahl als Erwachsene.  Das verkaufte Narrativ war Kinderschutz. Was gebaut wurde, ist Überwachungs-Infrastruktur. Und die Infrastruktur ist nicht mal sicher.  Europa, ich frage es ernst: Wer trägt die Verantwortung? Der Beamte, der sie bestellt hat, oder der Hacker, der sie in zehn Minuten zerlegt?  👇

🇷🇸 Serbia Data Leak – Personal Identification Data Exposed  A new post on a dark web forum claims a data leak involving individuals from Serbia, allegedly sourced from a hospitality-related entity in Zlatibor.  📊 Key Claims: • Dataset size: ~2,500 records • Data fields include:  First & last names JMBG (unique national ID number) Sample data shared publicly Full dataset offered via download link  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • JMBG exposure is highly sensitive — equivalent to a national identifier • Even small datasets (2.5K records) can be:  Used for identity theft Leveraged in targeted fraud / impersonation The wording (“we got ghosted”) suggests: Possible dispute-based leak (common in small breaches) Insider or partner-related exposure cannot be ruled out  ⚠️ Assessment: • Likely real, small-scale breach or data extraction • Impact is high per individual, even if total volume is low  ⚠️ Risk Implications: • Identity fraud and account takeovers • Social engineering using verified identity data • Long-term privacy impact due to immutable identifier (JMBG)  📊 Status: Unverified — but credible based on data structure and targeting pattern  ⸻  💬 Small datasets don’t mean small impact — especially when national IDs are involved.  #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #Serbia #DarkWeb #ThreatIntel #DDW

🇮🇳 Hacktivist Group Claims Breach of Indian Company (RAKASHA MARKETING PVT LTD)  A Telegram channel linked to “Anonymous Switzerland” claims to have compromised RAKASHA MARKETING PVT LTD, an Indian trading company, and released part of the data.  📊 Key Claims: • Alleged access to internal systems • Data exfiltration including:  Databases Financial documents Sensitive business information Partial data already shared (~68MB archive) More data reportedly to be released  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • This follows a typical hacktivist leak pattern:  Initial breach claim Small data drop as proof Pressure for attention or response Mid-sized companies are often targeted due to: Weaker security controls Lower visibility → easier compromise  ⚠️ Assessment: • No official confirmation • Partial data release suggests:  Possible real but limited access Or staged / selective leak  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of financial and operational data • Business disruption and reputational damage • Follow-on attacks using leaked information  📊 Status: Unverified — active leak situation developing  ⸻  💬 In modern breaches, the first leak is just the beginning — not the end.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DataBreach #Hacktivism #India #DarkWeb #DDW

🏥 Dubai Health Authority Data Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor claims to be selling data Dubai Health Authority (UAE), including a mix of personal, medical, and internal documents.  📊 Key Claims: • ~836 files  • Data allegedly includes:  Passports (multiple nationalities) Emirates IDs & visas Certifications (medical, academic, employment) Hospital/clinic architecture diagrams Personal signatures Government policy documents Internal reports and contracts  💰 Listing Details: • Price: ~$300 • Samples shared via external link  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Healthcare sector breaches are particularly sensitive due to:  Combination of PII + professional + infrastructure data Inclusion of: Architectural diagrams → potential physical security implications Identity documents → high fraud value  ⚠️ Assessment: • No official confirmation • Dataset size relatively small → could indicate:  Partial leak Curated or repackaged documents  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Identity theft and document abuse • Targeting of healthcare professionals • Exposure of sensitive facility information  📊 Status: Unverified — requires validation  ⸻  💬 In healthcare breaches, it’s not just data — it’s identity, infrastructure, and trust all at once.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #Healthcare #DataBreach #UAE #DarkWeb #DDW

☢️ Pakistan Nuclear Regulatory Authority (PNRA) Data Allegedly Compromised  A threat actor claims to have breached the Pakistan Nuclear Regulatory Authority (PNRA), offering multiple databases for sale.  📊 Key Claims: • Alleged access to mail server + 60+ databases • 17 databases (~3.2GB) currently listed for sale • Additional data reportedly held for future release  📄 Data Allegedly Includes: • Locations of nuclear-related facilities • Laboratory infrastructure details • Employee information and email addresses • Internal documents and sensitive records  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Claims involving nuclear or critical infrastructure are often:  Highly exaggerated Mixed with publicly available or outdated data However, even limited exposure could: Pose national security concerns Attract state-sponsored interest  ⚠️ Assessment: • No official confirmation of breach • High likelihood of:  Inflation of scope and sensitivity Requires careful validation before attribution  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of sensitive infrastructure-related data • Targeting of personnel • Geopolitical escalation if validated  📊 Status: Unverified — high-impact claim under monitoring  ⸻  💬 The more critical the target, the more skepticism the claim deserves.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #CriticalInfrastructure #Nuclear #DataBreach #DarkWeb #DDW

🇭🇺 KFC Hungary (kfc.hu) Customer Data Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has shared what appears to be customer records kfc.hu, including highly sensitive personal and account data.  📊 Exposed Data (Sample): • Email + password combinations • Full names • Phone numbers • Dates of birth • Physical addresses  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Presence of email + password pairs suggests:  Infostealer logs or credential harvesting Possible reuse from other breaches Food delivery / ordering platforms are often targeted due to: Stored personal + location data Frequent password reuse by users  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Account takeover (credential reuse) • Targeted phishing and fraud • Physical risk due to exposed addresses  📊 Status: Unverified — likely aggregated or infostealer-derived data  ⸻  💬 When login data meets real-world addresses, cyber risk becomes physical.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DataBreach #KFC #Privacy #DarkWeb #DDW

⛔️ Handala Main Telegram Channel Suspended  The primary Telegram channel associated with the hacktivist group Handala appears to have been taken down.  📊 Key Details: • Status: Suspended / inaccessible • Timing: Recently observed  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Takedowns like this are typically:  Platform enforcement actions Result of mass reporting or policy violations However, in most cases: Groups quickly migrate to backup channels Operations continue with minimal disruption  ⚠️ What to Expect: • Emergence of new or backup channels • Rebranding or shifting communication platforms • Continued activity despite takedown  📊 Status: Confirmed channel suspension — group activity likely ongoing elsewhere  ⸻  💬 In hacktivism, channels get suspended — operations don’t.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #Hacktivism #Telegram #DarkWeb #DDW

🏀 French Basketball Federation (FFBB) Data Allegedly for Sale (~2.7M Individuals)  A threat actor claims to be selling a dataset linked to the Fédération Française de Basketball (FFBB), impacting players and associated family members.  📊 Key Claims: • ~1.9M federation members • ~800K parent/guardian records • Includes:  Full names and addresses Emails and phone numbers Family-linked contact data  📈 Reported Stats: • ~1.9M full addresses • ~1.44M emails (members) • ~1.51M phone numbers (members) • Additional contact data for parents (emails & phones)  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Sports federations often hold:  Youth athlete data Family and guardian information This creates a multi-layered PII dataset, highly valuable for: Targeted phishing Social engineering involving families Fraud campaigns targeting minors’ networks  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of minors and family-linked identities • Highly targeted scams using relationship mapping • Long-term privacy impact across households  📊 Status: Unverified — based on underground forum listing  ⸻  💬 When datasets include families, the blast radius multiplies beyond individuals.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DataBreach #France #Privacy #DarkWeb #DDW

🎌 20 Million Japanese Citizen Records Allegedly for Sale  A threat actor is offering a massive “Japan population database” containing tens of millions of records on a dark web forum.  📊 Key Claims: • ~20 million records • Region: Japan • Format: CSV • Size: ~5GB (uncompressed)  📄 Data Allegedly Includes: • Names, dates of birth • Addresses and city-level details • Phone numbers • National ID-style identifiers  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Datasets labeled as “population databases” are often:  Aggregated from multiple leaks Mixed with public or scraped data However, at this scale, even partial accuracy enables: Mass profiling Highly targeted fraud campaigns  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Nationwide phishing and fraud operations • Identity theft at scale • Long-term exposure due to static personal data  📊 Status: Unverified — likely aggregated dataset  ⸻  💬 At population scale, even imperfect data becomes dangerously effective.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DataBreach #Japan #Privacy #DarkWeb #DDW

🇸🇻 Hacktivist Group Claims Breach of Claro El Salvador  A Telegram channel linked to “Anonymous Switzerland” claims to have compromised Claro El Salvador, a major telecom provider, alleging access to internal systems and large volumes of data.  📊 Key Claims: • Alleged access to internal network • ~200GB of data reportedly exfiltrated • Planned leak of ~5GB sensitive files (PDF, DOC, XLSX, DOCX) • Data includes:  Contracts User/customer data Internal corporate information  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Typical hacktivist playbook:  Initial claim → staged data release → media amplification Telecom operators are frequent targets due to: High-value customer data Strategic infrastructure relevance Claims often: Mix real access with exaggeration Use geopolitical narratives to amplify impact  ⚠️ Assessment: • No official confirmation from Claro • Data samples not independently verified • Likely scenario:  Either limited breach being exaggerated Or opportunistic claim leveraging geopolitical tensions  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of customer and internal telecom data • Targeted phishing / fraud campaigns • Service or reputational impact  📊 Status: Unverified — monitoring for validation or data release  ⸻  💬 In hacktivism, the announcement is just phase one — the real impact comes with proof.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #Hacktivism #DataBreach #Telecom #DarkWeb #DDW

🇵🇪 Alleged Admin Panel Access to Movistar Peru Exposed  A threat actor claims to have access to an administrator panel Movistar Peru, suggesting weak or missing access controls.  📊 Key Claims: • Admin panel access with ability to:  View user/customer information Manage accounts (update, disable, reset credentials) Interface shows: Names, emails, identifiers Account status and control actions  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • If legitimate, this indicates:  Broken access control / privilege misconfiguration Potential exposure of internal customer management systems Telecom platforms are high-value targets due to: SIM swap potential Account takeover and identity fraud  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Unauthorized account access and manipulation • Large-scale customer data exposure • SIM swap attacks and downstream financial fraud  📊 Status: Unverified — based on underground forum claim  ⸻  💬 When admin panels are exposed, attackers don’t need exploits — just access.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DataBreach #Telecom #AccessControl #DarkWeb #DDW

Russia raided nearly 100 suspected crypto criminals in a single day. What they found inside the apartments was not what anyone expected.  > Russian authorities were investigating an illegal crypto exchange network called Cryptex.  > Operating since 2013, Cryptex had processed over $1.4 BILLION in transactions.  > Their main clients were ransomware hackers, cybercriminals and money launderers across the world.  > In 2023 alone, the network moved over $1.1 BILLION and generated $38 MILLION in illegal income for those involved.  > On October 2, 2024, Russian federal investigators launched coordinated raids across 14 regions of the country.  > 148 locations searched. 96 people detained and transported to Moscow.  > This is what they seized:  > Robinson helicopters. Bentleys. Rolls Royces. Porsches. A Tesla Cybertruck. Boats. Snowmobiles. Bundles of cash.  > 1.5 BILLION rubles confiscated in a single day. Roughly $16 MILLION.  > Russian media called it the largest crypto crackdown in the country's history.  > The man at the top was Sergey Ivanov, known online as "Taleon."  > The US Treasury alleged he had been laundering crypto for ransomware operators, darknet vendors, and carding shops for approximately 20 years.  > The week before the Russian raids, the US had already moved.  > The Secret Service seized Cryptex's domains. The DOJ unsealed indictments against two Russian nationals.   > The State Department posted a bounty of up to $11 MILLION for information on Ivanov.  > Then Russia followed with the physical raids days later.  > Whether the two governments directly coordinated has never been confirmed.  > The suspects face up to 20 years in prison.  > One of the largest crypto laundering networks ever uncovered, hiding the proceeds in helicopters and luxury cars across 14 regions of Russia.  They laundered over a BILLION dollars for the world's most dangerous hackers. Then parked the evidence in their driveways.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-18 17:51 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 모델을 활용해 취약점을 탐색하고, 기존의 고전적인 피싱 기법을 Device Code Phishing과 같은 최신 인증 흐름을 악용하는 방식으로 고도화하고 있습니다. 특히, 기업 내 관리되지 않는 Non-human identities(서비스 계정, API 키)가 클라우드 침해의 주요 경로로 부상하고 있으며, 공급망 보안과 OT/ICS 환경에 대한 공격이 지속적으로 보고되고 있습니다. 또한, NIST의 NVD 운영 방식 변화로 인해 보안 팀의 취약점 관리 전략 재정립이 요구되는 시점입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **NoName057(16)** (ASEC): 대한민국 공공기관 및 기업 대상 DDoS 공격 수행.
*   **M-17SEC** (Twitter/X): 'OpsResurrect1' 캠페인의 일환으로 이스라엘 웹사이트(prpl.co.il) 대상 DDoS 공격 수행.
*   **Famous Chollima** (ASEC): 가짜 채용 인터뷰를 미끼로 한 북한 연계 APT 활동.
*   **Russian Military Intelligence Units** (Krebs on Security): 구형 라우터 취약점을 악용하여 Microsoft Office 인증 토큰 탈취.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **NoName057(16)**의 국내 공공기관 및 기업 대상 DDoS 공격 활동이 확인됨.
*   기업뱅킹 전자금융 소프트웨어(WGear) 취약점 관련 보안 권고 발생.
*   금융권 대상 피싱, 웹셸, 인포스틸러 유포 정황 및 텔레그램 API를 이용한 계정 유출 캠페인 관찰.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-35582**: Emissary OS Command Injection, CVSS 9.2, 현재 실제 공격 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-35616**: FortiClient EMS Pre-auth RCE, Critical, 인증되지 않은 공격자의 원격 코드 실행 가능.
*   **CVE-2024-3721**: TBK DVR/TP-Link 라우터 취약점, Mirai 변종(Nexcorium) 봇넷 구축에 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2023-33538**: TP-Link 라우터 Command Injection, Mirai 봇넷 페이로드 탐지됨.
*   **CVE-2026-39987**: Marimo Pre-Auth RCE, WebSocket을 통한 PTY 쉘 획득 가능.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**:
    *   CVE-2024-3721 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/mirai-variant-nexcorium-exploits-cve.html))
    *   CVE-2023-33538 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/exploitation-of-cve-2023-33538/))
*   **도메인/해시**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 직접적인 파일 해시나 C2 도메인 값은 명시되지 않음.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Non-human Identity 점검**: 기업 내 방치된 서비스 계정 및 API 키를 전수 조사하고, 사용하지 않는 계정은 즉시 비활성화하십시오.
*   **네트워크 장비 패치**: TBK DVR 및 TP-Link 라우터의 펌웨어를 최신 버전으로 업데이트하여 Mirai 변종 봇넷 감염을 차단하십시오.
*   **인증 보안 강화**: Device Code Phishing 방지를 위해 MFA 정책을 재검토하고, 사용자들에게 새로운 기기 로그인 흐름에 대한 보안 교육을 실시하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: 다수의 Critical 취약점(RCE)이 실제 공격에 악용되고 있으며, 국내 공공기관을 타겟으로 한 DDoS 공격과 금융권 대상 인포스틸러 유포가 활발함.
